project.name: FantasyPulse
project.summary: Fantasy sports product focused on lineup optimization and league engagement for casual and mid-core players.

product: Mobile-first fantasy sports companion for weekly lineup decisions.
Freemium model with premium analytics upsell.
audience: Casual fantasy players who want fast recommendations.
More engaged league players who care about weekly edge and bragging rights.
workflows: Join a league and connect roster context.
Check lineup recommendations before weekly lock.
Return for waiver and matchup insights during the season.
domain: Demand is seasonal and tied to league calendars.
Content and acquisition are driven by player news, matchups, and search intent.
business_model: Free tier plus paid subscription for premium insights.
growth: Priority is acquisition before season peaks and retention through weekly value.
constraints: Small founding team with limited paid acquisition budget.
assumptions: Current analytics maturity is low; attribution stack may be incomplete.

approved_facts.summary: User acquisition peaks from late August through September as league seasons start.
approved_facts.source: Founder confirmation after first launch cycle

preferences.summary: Prefer organic and partnership growth over aggressive paid acquisition until retention improves.

rejected_strategies.summary: Generic player-news recap content was rejected because it did not differentiate the product.

successful_patterns.summary: Weekly start-sit decision content produced the strongest engagement and referral sharing.

open_questions.summary: Best trigger for premium upgrade conversion is still unclear.

experts.title: SEO Lead
experts.summary: Owns search discovery and content architecture for seasonally driven acquisition.
mandate: Design organic growth strategy for fantasy-intent discovery.
external_usage: Use outside research only when validating keyword or competitor assumptions.

experts.title: Growth Lead
experts.summary: Owns growth diagnosis and experiment prioritization across acquisition, activation, and retention.
mandate: Identify the main growth bottleneck and maintain an experiment roadmap.
